HomeFundingA new line of defense: Cybersecurity startup Zynap raises €5.7 million for...
FundingSpain-Startups
A new line of defense: Cybersecurity startup Zynap raises €5.7 million for threat intelligence
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
January 21, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Zynap
Zynap, a Barcelona-based cybersecurity startup leveraging Gen-AI to fight cybercrime proactively by simulating cyber threat tactics, has announced its launch and close of their €5.7 million funding round to fuel their expansion plans.
The round was co-led by Kibo Ventures and Kfund, including key business angles. Together, they have provided the necessary capital to accelerate Zynap’s technology​ development and expansion plans.
“_Our mission is simple yet ambitious: Outsmarting Cybercrime. We believe cybercrime is a challenge that cannot be tackled with generic solutions but requires advanced technology, reliable data, and a disruptive approach_,” said Founder and CEO Daniel Solís.
Founded in 2024 by Daniel Solís, Zynap benefits from his experience as former CEO and founder of Blueliv and the backing of advisors like Pedro Castillo, founder of Onum and Devo, Spain’s first cybersecurity unicorn.
Zynap aims to redefine Threat Intelligence through technology that delivers actionable and hard-to-access operational insights. Its solutions empower enterprises and MSSPs (Managed Security Service Providers) to stay ahead of evolving digital threats. Zynap’s approach offers a practical, actionable focus, differentiating itself from existing market alternatives.
“_We’ve known the team for over a decade, and it’s inspiring to see them come together again, stronger and more experienced, around such a distinctive and essential product for the cybersecurity industry. We’re thrilled to invest in this team and their new venture for the second time_,” says Juan Santamaría, partner at Kibo Ventures.
“_We’re investing in Zynap because we see an extraordinary team with a clear vision and a product that solves real problems for large enterprises and MSSPs. We’re excited to be part of this journey,_” adds Jaime Novoa, partner at Kfund.
Zynap is designed to meet the needs of MSSPs and Fortune 2000 companies seeking agile, scalable, and effective tools to defend against cyberattacks. In a landscape where threats constantly evolve, Zynap looks to deliver innovative, market-differentiating solutions.
Its standardised solutions ensure integration with existing tools and frameworks while offering customisation to suit specific client and sector needs. Zynap provides measurable outcomes that empower clients to make informed decisions.
Additionally, by leveraging generative AI alongside other advanced technologies, Zynap reportedly delivers precise results that enable businesses to protect themselves from cybercriminal threats even before they occur.
Zynap’s team is currently developing a trial version of its product, set to be released in the coming months.

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingSpain's HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI,...
FundingSpain-Startups
Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share [...] Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.

Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
Tucuvi, a Madrid-based HealthTech company innovating certified voice AI, raised €17 million ($20 million) in Series A funding to accelerate the adoption of its AI-based care management platform in healthcare systems.
The round was led by Cathay Innovation and Kfund, through its early-growth fund Leadwind, with participation from existing investors Frontline Ventures, Seaya Ventures, and Shilling.
“Healthcare is under enormous pressure, and partial solutions are no longer enough,” explains María González, CEO of Tucuvi. “To continue providing quality care at scale, healthcare systems need AI they can trust: secure, auditable, and designed for real clinical environments. We are proud to already be working with more than 60 healthcare organisations and continue to grow to meet increasing demand.” (Translated).
Tucuvi’s Series A round can be situated within a wider, still relatively selective flow of European capital into AI-driven healthcare platforms during 2025–2026.
In early 2025, Warsaw-based primary care startup Jutro Medical raised €12 million in a Series A round to expand its network of physical clinics and deploy medical AI agents across telemedicine and operational workflows. This was followed later in the year by a €24 million Series A extension, also reported by EU-Startups, to further scale its AI-first primary care model and automation capabilities across clinical and administrative functions.
Against this backdrop, Tucuvi’s Madrid-based focus on certified voice AI and end-to-end care management workflows reflects a parallel approach to addressing workforce shortages and care complexity, albeit through software deployed directly into existing healthcare systems rather than through direct care delivery.
Taken together, the roughly €53 million raised across these announcements points to a growing, though still concentrated, level of investment in European healthcare AI companies seeking to increase care capacity and operational efficiency through automation.
“The Tucuvi platform transforms protocols and clinical guidelines into real, reliable, and consistent AI workflows, allowing us to interact with all our patients and enabling our nursing team to devote their expertise to cases that require high-value clinical judgment,”  adds an unidentified Medical Director. (Translated).
Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.
Tucuvi currently supports more than 50 workflows, ranging from post-surgical follow-up and discharge to chronic disease management, preoperative assessments, test preparation, screening programs, appointment scheduling, and demand management.
Tucuvi’s voice AI agents handle phone interactions, while the platform as a whole takes care of workflow logic, referrals, documentation, and integration with existing healthcare system operations.
“What convinced us at Cathay Innovation is Tucuvi’s ability to combine clinical rigor, regulatory credibility, and real-world scalability in a single platform,” said Jacky Abitbol, Managing Partner at Cathay Innovation. “Its approach allows healthcare organisations to deploy autonomous AI with confidence, freeing up significant capacity for care teams while maintaining the highest levels of safety and quality. We believe Tucuvi is blazing a new trail in how AI can be responsibly integrated into healthcare operations.”
Tucuvi is reportedly the first AI platform to obtain European regulatory approval as a Class IIb Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for both its voice agent and patient management platform.
The company outlines that their platform has generated consistent and measurable results in collaborating healthcare systems, including:
 Up to 80% automation of nursing follow-up flows
 Patient reach and adherence greater than 90%, even in highly complex populations
 Improved outcomes in chronic diseases, such as a 5.5% reduction in readmissions for COPD
With this funding, Tucuvi will accelerate its growth strategy in Europe and the United States, expand its AI capabilities, and continue to evolve its platform as a comprehensive action and intelligence system for care teams.

FundingSpain-Startups
Spain’s Zepo Intelligence raises €12.8 million to protect workplaces from AI-driven human-targeted cyber threats
Image 8: Rahul Raj
ByRahul Raj
January 12, 2026
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Zepo-Intelligence
Spain-based Zepo Intelligence, a cybersecurity startup protecting organisations from AI-powered social engineering, has today announced the closing of a €12.8 million ($15 million) Seed investment round.
The round was backed by Kibo Ventures, eCAPITAL, and TIN Capital. The fresh capital will be used to scale its proprietary technology globally and expand its team, with a focus on data engineering and artificial intelligence roles.
_“AI is the core of our platform. For engineers looking to develop their careers at the frontier of AI and cybersecurity, Zepo offers the ideal environment to build technology with global impact,”_ explained Enrique Holgado, CPTO and co-founder of Zepo.
Founded in 2021 by Antonio Muñoz and Enrique Holgado, Zepo Intelligence claims to be the first agentic social intelligence platform for workspace security. It enables organisations to simulate real-world, AI-driven social engineering attacks (deepfakes, voice calls, cross-platform messages, or personalised phishing), identify human vulnerabilities. It also provides metrics and training adapted to each employee’s behaviour, to empower team capabilities.
According to Zepo, the rise of generative AI has rendered traditional cybersecurity involving isolated training tools and email filters obsolete. Citing a World Economic Forum report, the company mentioned that since the launch of ChatGPT, social engineering attacks have surged by 1,200%, resulting in over $1 trillion (nearly €860 billion) in losses in 2024 alone.
Antonio Muñoz, CEO and co-founder of Zepo Intelligence, explained, _“Artificial Intelligence has turned social engineering into one of the biggest challenges society faces today. As attackers have exploited generative AI and moved to multi-vector personalised attacks on employees, traditional cybersecurity – focused on isolated training tools and email filters – no longer provides the needed protection. Zepo responds to this gap by leading Agentic Social Intelligence for Workspace Security._
_“The most serious security failures today don’t begin with systems. They begin with people. Organisations need defences that understand human behaviour in real time, not incident reports after the damage is done. Zepo’s platform goes beyond training and simulations by combining human risk management with live threat detection. In a world where employees can’t reliably spot deepfakes or AI-crafted manipulation, protection has to show up in the moment. Just before a message turns into a transfer, a credential leak, or a breach. The result is a unified, proactive layer that helps protect employees across the channels where modern work actually happens.”_
Zepo Intelligence was founded in Southern Europe and currently has teams distributed across New York, Madrid, Tel Aviv, and Mexico City. The startup has reported 5x year-over-year revenue growth and plans to increase the team and expand operations in Europe and the US over the next 18 months. The company has also been recognised by Google as one of the 15 most innovative cybersecurity and AI startups globally.
   Tags
   Antonio Muñoz
   cybersecurity startups
   eCapital
   Enrique Holgado
   Kibo Ventures
   social engineering
   Spain
   TIN Capital
   Zepo Intelligence

HomeFundingMadrid's Anyformat closes €3.3 million round to scale its agentic OCR and...
FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid’s Anyformat closes €3.3 million round to scale its agentic OCR and data-sovereignty-focused document tools
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
November 27, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Anyformat
Anyformat, a Spanish GenAI platform specialising in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a Seed round of €3.3 million to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models.
The round was led by Kibo Ventures and followed by 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures and Decelera Ventures, funds that already participated in the previous round.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says, “_Documents are the backbone of any organisation. Our goal is to enable companies to analyse them with human intelligence, machine speed and the security required by regulation. We are building the new European document infrastructure.‘_(Translated).
Among European startups working on document processing, data extraction and AI-enabled enterprise automation, 2025 funding activity has been limited but illustrative: France-based Klaimy raised €1.2 million to advance its AI assistant for processing medical documentation, while Prague-headquartered Resistant AI secured €21 million in Series B financing to strengthen its document-fraud detection and financial-crime prevention systems.
Together, these comparable 2025 rounds amount to roughly €22.2 million, indicating that investor attention in this domain remains focused on specialised applications such as insurance, medical underwriting and fraud detection rather than broad, general-purpose document-infrastructure platforms.
In this context, Anyformat’s €3.3 million Seed roundpositions it as one of the few European companies pursuing a horizontal, generative-AI-based document-intelligence layer – particularly notable given the absence of other Spain-based fundraises in the category – adding meaningful weight to the still-emerging European document-automation landscape.
Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds:_“We are developing technology that goes far beyond document digitisation_.” (Translated)
Founded in 2024, Anyformat is innovating a new generation of document infrastructure based on generative AI, which reportedly allows the operational teams of large corporations to work with complex documents with human precision and machine speed, while maintaining control of the data at all times.
Since its previous round, where it raised €520k in pre-Seed funding (as covered by EU-Startups), Anyformat has expanded its offering beyond classic extraction. Today, the company says they are the only provider that allows the information extracted from a document to be combined in real time with internal and proprietary company data.
This means that a contract, invoice or email can be analysed automatically while being cross-referenced directly with corporate databases, such as customers, policies, catalogues or history.
For organisations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors and accelerates critical business processes.
This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to convert their data into valuable information in real time.
“_The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe._
_“The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest_,” shares Javier Torremocha, co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in ARR in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance and advanced document processing.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001.
The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organisations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control.

Logo
Spanish government raises €67 million for Multiverse Computing for AI compression
David Cendon Garcia
Multiverse-Computing

Spanish government raises €67 million for Multiverse Computing for AI compression
David Cendon Garcia
Multiverse-Computing
Spanish Minister for Digital Transformation and Public Function, Óscar López, announced on Tuesday that the Government of Spain will become a shareholder of the Donostia-based company Multiverse Computing, with a co-investment of €67 million.
The operation will be carried out through the Spanish Society for Technological Transformation (SETT).
Rodrigo Hernandez, Global GenAI Director at Multiverse Computing, will also be attending the EU-Startups Summit 2025 in Malta as one of our Speakers, read more here.
“We appreciate the support of the Spanish government and will work to make Spain and Europe leaders in this technology. Also, support from the Basque government has helped us achieve the success we have today,” said Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Co-founder and CEO of Multiverse Computing. “We are guiding the next generation of AI algorithms by making these models smaller and more efficient to lower operating costs and reduce the environmental impact.”
Founded in 2019 by Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Román Orús, Samuel Mugel, and Alfonso Rubio, Multiverse Computing has developed software inspired by quantum computing that allows AI models to be compressed to 10% of the original size, consuming fewer resources and obtaining equivalent returns.
This allows, for example, to use these models using less energy or on smaller devices, such as virtual reality glasses or smartphones instead of large computers, even in offline mode.
Their mission is to apply quantum and quantum-inspired AI to solve complex problems in finance, energy, manufacturing, defense, cybersecurity, life sciences, and chemistry, delivering practical applications and tangible value.
They look to provide efficient software to companies wanting to gain an edge with quantum computing and AI.
One of their flagship products is Singularity, a software platform that contains quantum and quantum-inspired algorithms developed and patented through proof-of-concept trials we have been performing for industrial and service clients.
Their other product CompactifAI, is an AI model compression tool that reduces AI model size while maintaining performance, leading to lower energy consumption and costs. CompactifAI is meant for real-time processing and edge computing applications. So far, it has been adopted by enterprises in healthcare, automotive, and telecommunications.
“With this operation, we seek to position Spain as a benchmark in energy-efficient AI language models, scaling the development of compaction tools that compress language models by up to 90%, reducing costs and energy consumption while increasing the technological independence of Spain and Europe,” said Minister López at a press conference in the Spanish Pavilion at MWC Barcelona 2025.
The Minister also announced new grants aimed at helping companies incorporate AI into their value chains. Firstly, a new call for €130 million from ERDF funds. The grants, of up to €5 million per project, will be articulated through the Red.es entity and “will allow our companies to launch experimental development projects in which intensive use will be made of technologies associated with AI, such as machine learning and deep learning, generative AI or LLMs, among others.”
In addition, Minister López announced another call for €50 million euros for the integration of AI in companies in the health sector; both, added to the call to promote the use of AI and data in Spanish SMEs, closed a few days ago and which has a budget of €24 million, “are more than €200 million of European funds–ERDF and RTRP–that will be activated in the coming days and that will allow us to continue exploiting our strengths in the field of Artificial Intelligence,” said the Minister.
López made these announcements at MWC Barcelona 2025 during his speech at the event ‘CambIA: from research to business,’ in which some of the results of the ‘R+D Missions in AI’ programme were announced. This initiative, endowed with €50 million from the RTRP and launched by the Secretary of State for Digitalisation and Artificial Intelligence (SEDIA) in 2021, concluded last December and has developed real use cases for the application of AI in strategic sectors of the country’s economy.

FundingSpain-Startups
A new line of defense: Cybersecurity startup Zynap raises €5.7 million for threat intelligence
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
January 21, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Zynap
Zynap, a Barcelona-based cybersecurity startup leveraging Gen-AI to fight cybercrime proactively by simulating cyber threat tactics, has announced its launch and close of their €5.7 million funding round to fuel their expansion plans.
The round was co-led by Kibo Ventures and Kfund, including key business angles. Together, they have provided the necessary capital to accelerate Zynap’s technology​ development and expansion plans.
“_Our mission is simple yet ambitious: Outsmarting Cybercrime. We believe cybercrime is a challenge that cannot be tackled with generic solutions but requires advanced technology, reliable data, and a disruptive approach_,” said Founder and CEO Daniel Solís.
Founded in 2024 by Daniel Solís, Zynap benefits from his experience as former CEO and founder of Blueliv and the backing of advisors like Pedro Castillo, founder of Onum and Devo, Spain’s first cybersecurity unicorn.
Zynap aims to redefine Threat Intelligence through technology that delivers actionable and hard-to-access operational insights. Its solutions empower enterprises and MSSPs (Managed Security Service Providers) to stay ahead of evolving digital threats. Zynap’s approach offers a practical, actionable focus, differentiating itself from existing market alternatives.
“_We’ve known the team for over a decade, and it’s inspiring to see them come together again, stronger and more experienced, around such a distinctive and essential product for the cybersecurity industry. We’re thrilled to invest in this team and their new venture for the second time_,” says Juan Santamaría, partner at Kibo Ventures.
“_We’re investing in Zynap because we see an extraordinary team with a clear vision and a product that solves real problems for large enterprises and MSSPs. We’re excited to be part of this journey,_” adds Jaime Novoa, partner at Kfund.
Zynap is designed to meet the needs of MSSPs and Fortune 2000 companies seeking agile, scalable, and effective tools to defend against cyberattacks. In a landscape where threats constantly evolve, Zynap looks to deliver innovative, market-differentiating solutions.
Its standardised solutions ensure integration with existing tools and frameworks while offering customisation to suit specific client and sector needs. Zynap provides measurable outcomes that empower clients to make informed decisions.
Additionally, by leveraging generative AI alongside other advanced technologies, Zynap reportedly delivers precise results that enable businesses to protect themselves from cybercriminal threats even before they occur.
Zynap’s team is currently developing a trial version of its product, set to be released in the coming months.

HomeFundingWith €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand...
FundingSpain-Startups
With €3.5 million in funding, Spanish startup Omnia is helping brands stand out across AI platforms
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
October 16, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Omnia
EU-Startups has learned that Madrid-based Omnia, an agentic platform that helps brands boost their presence across AI engines and assistants, has raised €3.5 million in pre-Seed financing to accelerate their product roadmap, fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion.
The round was led by Visionaries Club, with participation from KFund and Baobab Ventures. Angel investors include Carles Reina (ElevenLabs), Paulo Rodriguez (Vanta) and Hugo Arévalo (ThePower).
“_AI assistants are becoming the front door to the internet_,” says Daniel Espejo, CEO and Co-founder of Omnia. “_Omnia gives teams the playbook and the agents to act on it. Moving from dashboards to done. Just like Cursor makes engineers 10× more productive, Omnia makes marketers 10× more effective on the channels that matter now_.”
Omnia’s pre-Seed round aligns with a growing wave of European investment into agentic and AI-driven marketing platforms in 2025.
Comparable early-stage rounds include Massive Dynamic (€3 million, Paris) and Tandem (€3.2 million, Paris), both developing AI-based orchestration tools. Further up the scale, Sweden’s EvoluteIQ secured €44 million to expand its enterprise agentic-AI suite, while Italy’s SylloTips (€4.2 million) and the Netherlands’ Aizy (€1.5 million) also closed funding to advance AI-based marketing and agent platforms.
Within this context, there haven’t been many comparable funding rounds in Spain, positioning Omnia as an early mover in Spain’s emerging agentic-AI marketing ecosystem.
“_As search shifts rapidly from browsers to AI engines and assistants, every brand will need an agentic strategy to stay visible,_” saidRobert Jaeckle, Partner at Visionaries Club. “_Omnia is pioneering this space by building the agentic platform that turns visibility into action for marketeers. We’re so excited to partner with Daniel and Miguel as they define this new category for Europe and beyond._”
Omnia was founded in 2025 by Daniel Espejo (CEO), formerly at Klarna where he launched markets and scaled products used by millions, and Miguel Fernández (CTO), a platform engineer with experience at GitHub and Fastly.
Omnia is an agentic platform for AI search visibility, helping brands discover high-demand topics in AI ​assistants, monitor their positioning, understand the sources those assistants cite, and launch agents to create and place AI-optimised content where it matters.
The company enables marketing and growth teams to influence how AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews answer customer questions.
The platform:
   Surfaces the most searched topics relevant to a brand.
   Benchmarks how a brand performs vs. competitors.
   Reveals the sources AI engines cite.
   Launches agents that create and place AI-optimized content in the right sources, closing the loop from insights to action.
Since launch, Omnia has been adopted by brands including Personio, ElevenLabs, Embat, Línea Directa, Ironhack, Air Europa, Praktika.ai , Trade Republic, Pleo and HappyRobot. ​
Also by marketing agencies like MIO group, Elogia, Product Hackers and Everybody. Early customers are allegedly reporting up to 3× visibility gains across AI channels.
“_Omnia has been key to our growth – helping us understand what potential clients are searching for, how well we’re positioned, and how to capture leads from AI across different markets,_” shared Adrian Varez, Head of Growth at Happy Robot.
The new funding will accelerate Omnia’s product roadmap (expanding topic intelligence, source coverage, and agentic placement), fuel commercial growth, and support international expansion from its base in Madrid, Spain.

HomeFundingSpain's HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI,...
FundingSpain-Startups
Spain’s HealthTech startup Tucuvi raises €17 million to scale LOLA voice AI, reporting up to 80% automation in nursing follow-up
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
Tucuvi, a Madrid-based HealthTech company innovating certified voice AI, raised €17 million ($20 million) in Series A funding to accelerate the adoption of its AI-based care management platform in healthcare systems.
The round was led by Cathay Innovation and Kfund, through its early-growth fund Leadwind, with participation from existing investors Frontline Ventures, Seaya Ventures, and Shilling.
“Healthcare is under enormous pressure, and partial solutions are no longer enough,” explains María González, CEO of Tucuvi. “To continue providing quality care at scale, healthcare systems need AI they can trust: secure, auditable, and designed for real clinical environments. We are proud to already be working with more than 60 healthcare organisations and continue to grow to meet increasing demand.” (Translated).
Tucuvi’s Series A round can be situated within a wider, still relatively selective flow of European capital into AI-driven healthcare platforms during 2025–2026.
In early 2025, Warsaw-based primary care startup Jutro Medical raised €12 million in a Series A round to expand its network of physical clinics and deploy medical AI agents across telemedicine and operational workflows. This was followed later in the year by a €24 million Series A extension, also reported by EU-Startups, to further scale its AI-first primary care model and automation capabilities across clinical and administrative functions.
Against this backdrop, Tucuvi’s Madrid-based focus on certified voice AI and end-to-end care management workflows reflects a parallel approach to addressing workforce shortages and care complexity, albeit through software deployed directly into existing healthcare systems rather than through direct care delivery.
Taken together, the roughly €53 million raised across these announcements points to a growing, though still concentrated, level of investment in European healthcare AI companies seeking to increase care capacity and operational efficiency through automation.
“The Tucuvi platform transforms protocols and clinical guidelines into real, reliable, and consistent AI workflows, allowing us to interact with all our patients and enabling our nursing team to devote their expertise to cases that require high-value clinical judgment,”  adds an unidentified Medical Director. (Translated).
Healthcare systems are facing increasing pressure from a shortage of professionals, rising demand for care, and greater operational complexity. Tucuvi was founded in 2019 to respond to this context with secure, auditable, and autonomous AI agents that aim to expand care capacity without compromising clinical quality or trust.
The company has developed an AI-powered Care Management platform designed to automate and orchestrate high-volume care workflows from start to finish.
The platform is based on AI agents, including LOLA, a voice-based AI agent that conducts secure phone conversations with patients, executes clinical and care coordination workflows, and escalates to human teams when necessary.
Tucuvi currently supports more than 50 workflows, ranging from post-surgical follow-up and discharge to chronic disease management, preoperative assessments, test preparation, screening programs, appointment scheduling, and demand management.
Tucuvi’s voice AI agents handle phone interactions, while the platform as a whole takes care of workflow logic, referrals, documentation, and integration with existing healthcare system operations.
“What convinced us at Cathay Innovation is Tucuvi’s ability to combine clinical rigor, regulatory credibility, and real-world scalability in a single platform,” said Jacky Abitbol, Managing Partner at Cathay Innovation. “Its approach allows healthcare organisations to deploy autonomous AI with confidence, freeing up significant capacity for care teams while maintaining the highest levels of safety and quality. We believe Tucuvi is blazing a new trail in how AI can be responsibly integrated into healthcare operations.”
Tucuvi is reportedly the first AI platform to obtain European regulatory approval as a Class IIb Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for both its voice agent and patient management platform.
The company outlines that their platform has generated consistent and measurable results in collaborating healthcare systems, including:
 Up to 80% automation of nursing follow-up flows
 Patient reach and adherence greater than 90%, even in highly complex populations
 Improved outcomes in chronic diseases, such as a 5.5% reduction in readmissions for COPD
With this funding, Tucuvi will accelerate its growth strategy in Europe and the United States, expand its AI capabilities, and continue to evolve its platform as a comprehensive action and intelligence system for care teams.

HomeSpain-StartupsSpain’s Zepo Intelligence raises €12.8 million to protect workplaces from AI-driven human-targeted...
FundingSpain-Startups
Spain’s Zepo Intelligence raises €12.8 million to protect workplaces from AI-driven human-targeted cyber threats
Image 8: Rahul Raj
ByRahul Raj
January 12, 2026
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Zepo-Intelligence
Spain-based Zepo Intelligence, a cybersecurity startup protecting organisations from AI-powered social engineering, has today announced the closing of a €12.8 million ($15 million) Seed investment round.
The round was backed by Kibo Ventures, eCAPITAL, and TIN Capital. The fresh capital will be used to scale its proprietary technology globally and expand its team, with a focus on data engineering and artificial intelligence roles.
_“AI is the core of our platform. For engineers looking to develop their careers at the frontier of AI and cybersecurity, Zepo offers the ideal environment to build technology with global impact,”_ explained Enrique Holgado, CPTO and co-founder of Zepo.
Founded in 2021 by Antonio Muñoz and Enrique Holgado, Zepo Intelligence claims to be the first agentic social intelligence platform for workspace security. It enables organisations to simulate real-world, AI-driven social engineering attacks (deepfakes, voice calls, cross-platform messages, or personalised phishing), identify human vulnerabilities. It also provides metrics and training adapted to each employee’s behaviour, to empower team capabilities.
According to Zepo, the rise of generative AI has rendered traditional cybersecurity involving isolated training tools and email filters obsolete. Citing a World Economic Forum report, the company mentioned that since the launch of ChatGPT, social engineering attacks have surged by 1,200%, resulting in over $1 trillion (nearly €860 billion) in losses in 2024 alone.
Antonio Muñoz, CEO and co-founder of Zepo Intelligence, explained, _“Artificial Intelligence has turned social engineering into one of the biggest challenges society faces today. As attackers have exploited generative AI and moved to multi-vector personalised attacks on employees, traditional cybersecurity – focused on isolated training tools and email filters – no longer provides the needed protection. Zepo responds to this gap by leading Agentic Social Intelligence for Workspace Security._
_“The most serious security failures today don’t begin with systems. They begin with people. Organisations need defences that understand human behaviour in real time, not incident reports after the damage is done. Zepo’s platform goes beyond training and simulations by combining human risk management with live threat detection. In a world where employees can’t reliably spot deepfakes or AI-crafted manipulation, protection has to show up in the moment. Just before a message turns into a transfer, a credential leak, or a breach. The result is a unified, proactive layer that helps protect employees across the channels where modern work actually happens.”_
Zepo Intelligence was founded in Southern Europe and currently has teams distributed across New York, Madrid, Tel Aviv, and Mexico City. The startup has reported 5x year-over-year revenue growth and plans to increase the team and expand operations in Europe and the US over the next 18 months. The company has also been recognised by Google as one of the 15 most innovative cybersecurity and AI startups globally.
   Tags
   Antonio Muñoz

   Enrique Holgado

HomeFundingMadrid's Anyformat closes €3.3 million round to scale its agentic OCR and...
FundingSpain-Startups
Madrid’s Anyformat closes €3.3 million round to scale its agentic OCR and data-sovereignty-focused document tools
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
November 27, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Anyformat
Anyformat, a Spanish GenAI platform specialising in extracting and structuring complex data from any document, has closed a Seed round of €3.3 million to expand the engineering and product team, driving its roadmap in advanced extraction capabilities and the development of proprietary error detection models.
The round was led by Kibo Ventures and followed by 4Founders, Abac Nest Ventures and Decelera Ventures, funds that already participated in the previous round.
Juan Huguet, CEO and co-founder of Anyformat, says, “_Documents are the backbone of any organisation. Our goal is to enable companies to analyse them with human intelligence, machine speed and the security required by regulation. We are building the new European document infrastructure.‘_(Translated).
Among European startups working on document processing, data extraction and AI-enabled enterprise automation, 2025 funding activity has been limited but illustrative: France-based Klaimy raised €1.2 million to advance its AI assistant for processing medical documentation, while Prague-headquartered Resistant AI secured €21 million in Series B financing to strengthen its document-fraud detection and financial-crime prevention systems.
Together, these comparable 2025 rounds amount to roughly €22.2 million, indicating that investor attention in this domain remains focused on specialised applications such as insurance, medical underwriting and fraud detection rather than broad, general-purpose document-infrastructure platforms.
In this context, Anyformat’s €3.3 million Seed roundpositions it as one of the few European companies pursuing a horizontal, generative-AI-based document-intelligence layer – particularly notable given the absence of other Spain-based fundraises in the category – adding meaningful weight to the still-emerging European document-automation landscape.
Diego Pérez, co-founder and CTO of the company, adds:_“We are developing technology that goes far beyond document digitisation_.” (Translated)
Founded in 2024, Anyformat is innovating a new generation of document infrastructure based on generative AI, which reportedly allows the operational teams of large corporations to work with complex documents with human precision and machine speed, while maintaining control of the data at all times.
Since its previous round, where it raised €520k in pre-Seed funding (as covered by EU-Startups), Anyformat has expanded its offering beyond classic extraction. Today, the company says they are the only provider that allows the information extracted from a document to be combined in real time with internal and proprietary company data.
This means that a contract, invoice or email can be analysed automatically while being cross-referenced directly with corporate databases, such as customers, policies, catalogues or history.
For organisations with complex documentation and fragmented systems, this capability eliminates manual validations, reduces errors and accelerates critical business processes.
This represents a real problem: according to Dell Technologies’ Innovation Catalyst report, 68% of Spanish companies are unable to convert their data into valuable information in real time.
“_The document intelligence market is entering a new phase driven by generative AI and agentic systems. Anyformat has developed a unique technology that allows companies to automate critical processes while maintaining data sovereignty, a key issue in Europe._
_“The vision and talent of the founders, whom we know from their time at Clarity.ai, a company also invested in by Kibo Ventures, combined with the possibilities offered by AI in a huge market, were key factors in our decision to invest_,” shares Javier Torremocha, co-founder and Managing Partner of Kibo Ventures.
Anyformat aims to exceed €1 million in ARR in 2026, supported by its commercial expansion in Europe and the increase in use cases linked to automation, compliance and advanced document processing.
In addition, the company will continue to invest in its privacy-first infrastructure, strengthening security processes and advancing certifications such as ISO 27001.
The company is positioning its Agentic OCR as the new standard for automating critical processes in organisations that cannot compromise on accuracy or data control.

David Cendon Garcia
Multiverse-Computing
Spanish Minister for Digital Transformation and Public Function, Óscar López, announced on Tuesday that the Government of Spain will become a shareholder of the Donostia-based company Multiverse Computing, with a co-investment of €67 million.
The operation will be carried out through the Spanish Society for Technological Transformation (SETT).
Rodrigo Hernandez, Global GenAI Director at Multiverse Computing, will also be attending the EU-Startups Summit 2025 in Malta as one of our Speakers, read more here. [...] Their other product CompactifAI, is an AI model compression tool that reduces AI model size while maintaining performance, leading to lower energy consumption and costs. CompactifAI is meant for real-time processing and edge computing applications. So far, it has been adopted by enterprises in healthcare, automotive, and telecommunications.
“With this operation, we seek to position Spain as a benchmark in energy-efficient AI language models, scaling the development of compaction tools that compress language models by up to 90%, reducing costs and energy consumption while increasing the technological independence of Spain and Europe,” said Minister López at a press conference in the Spanish Pavilion at MWC Barcelona 2025. [...] “We appreciate the support of the Spanish government and will work to make Spain and Europe leaders in this technology. Also, support from the Basque government has helped us achieve the success we have today,” said Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Co-founder and CEO of Multiverse Computing. “We are guiding the next generation of AI algorithms by making these models smaller and more efficient to lower operating costs and reduce the environmental impact.”
Founded in 2019 by Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Román Orús, Samuel Mugel, and Alfonso Rubio, Multiverse Computing has developed software inspired by quantum computing that allows AI models to be compressed to 10% of the original size, consuming fewer resources and obtaining equivalent returns.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Logo
Logo
Logo

Logo
Spanish government raises €67 million for Multiverse Computing for AI compression
David Cendon Garcia
Multiverse-Computing
Spanish Minister for Digital Transformation and Public Function, Óscar López, announced on Tuesday that the Government of Spain will become a shareholder of the Donostia-based company Multiverse Computing, with a co-investment of €67 million.
The operation will be carried out through the Spanish Society for Technological Transformation (SETT).
Rodrigo Hernandez, Global GenAI Director at Multiverse Computing, will also be attending the EU-Startups Summit 2025 in Malta as one of our Speakers, read more here.
“We appreciate the support of the Spanish government and will work to make Spain and Europe leaders in this technology. Also, support from the Basque government has helped us achieve the success we have today,” said Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Co-founder and CEO of Multiverse Computing. “We are guiding the next generation of AI algorithms by making these models smaller and more efficient to lower operating costs and reduce the environmental impact.”
Founded in 2019 by Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Román Orús, Samuel Mugel, and Alfonso Rubio, Multiverse Computing has developed software inspired by quantum computing that allows AI models to be compressed to 10% of the original size, consuming fewer resources and obtaining equivalent returns.
This allows, for example, to use these models using less energy or on smaller devices, such as virtual reality glasses or smartphones instead of large computers, even in offline mode.
Their mission is to apply quantum and quantum-inspired AI to solve complex problems in finance, energy, manufacturing, defense, cybersecurity, life sciences, and chemistry, delivering practical applications and tangible value.
They look to provide efficient software to companies wanting to gain an edge with quantum computing and AI.
One of their flagship products is Singularity, a software platform that contains quantum and quantum-inspired algorithms developed and patented through proof-of-concept trials we have been performing for industrial and service clients.
Their other product CompactifAI, is an AI model compression tool that reduces AI model size while maintaining performance, leading to lower energy consumption and costs. CompactifAI is meant for real-time processing and edge computing applications. So far, it has been adopted by enterprises in healthcare, automotive, and telecommunications.
“With this operation, we seek to position Spain as a benchmark in energy-efficient AI language models, scaling the development of compaction tools that compress language models by up to 90%, reducing costs and energy consumption while increasing the technological independence of Spain and Europe,” said Minister López at a press conference in the Spanish Pavilion at MWC Barcelona 2025.
The Minister also announced new grants aimed at helping companies incorporate AI into their value chains. Firstly, a new call for €130 million from ERDF funds. The grants, of up to €5 million per project, will be articulated through the Red.es entity and “will allow our companies to launch experimental development projects in which intensive use will be made of technologies associated with AI, such as machine learning and deep learning, generative AI or LLMs, among others.”
In addition, Minister López announced another call for €50 million euros for the integration of AI in companies in the health sector; both, added to the call to promote the use of AI and data in Spanish SMEs, closed a few days ago and which has a budget of €24 million, “are more than €200 million of European funds–ERDF and RTRP–that will be activated in the coming days and that will allow us to continue exploiting our strengths in the field of Artificial Intelligence,” said the Minister.
López made these announcements at MWC Barcelona 2025 during his speech at the event ‘CambIA: from research to business,’ in which some of the results of the ‘R+D Missions in AI’ programme were announced. This initiative, endowed with €50 million from the RTRP and launched by the Secretary of State for Digitalisation and Artificial Intelligence (SEDIA) in 2021, concluded last December and has developed real use cases for the application of AI in strategic sectors of the country’s economy.
David Cendon Garcia

HomeFundingA new line of defense: Cybersecurity startup Zynap raises €5.7 million for...
FundingSpain-Startups
A new line of defense: Cybersecurity startup Zynap raises €5.7 million for threat intelligence
Image 8: David Cendon Garcia
ByDavid Cendon Garcia
January 21, 2025
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin

Image 9: Zynap
Zynap, a Barcelona-based cybersecurity startup leveraging Gen-AI to fight cybercrime proactively by simulating cyber threat tactics, has announced its launch and close of their €5.7 million funding round to fuel their expansion plans.
The round was co-led by Kibo Ventures and Kfund, including key business angles. Together, they have provided the necessary capital to accelerate Zynap’s technology​ development and expansion plans.
“_Our mission is simple yet ambitious: Outsmarting Cybercrime. We believe cybercrime is a challenge that cannot be tackled with generic solutions but requires advanced technology, reliable data, and a disruptive approach_,” said Founder and CEO Daniel Solís.
Founded in 2024 by Daniel Solís, Zynap benefits from his experience as former CEO and founder of Blueliv and the backing of advisors like Pedro Castillo, founder of Onum and Devo, Spain’s first cybersecurity unicorn.
Zynap aims to redefine Threat Intelligence through technology that delivers actionable and hard-to-access operational insights. Its solutions empower enterprises and MSSPs (Managed Security Service Providers) to stay ahead of evolving digital threats. Zynap’s approach offers a practical, actionable focus, differentiating itself from existing market alternatives.
“_We’ve known the team for over a decade, and it’s inspiring to see them come together again, stronger and more experienced, around such a distinctive and essential product for the cybersecurity industry. We’re thrilled to invest in this team and their new venture for the second time_,” says Juan Santamaría, partner at Kibo Ventures.
“_We’re investing in Zynap because we see an extraordinary team with a clear vision and a product that solves real problems for large enterprises and MSSPs. We’re excited to be part of this journey,_” adds Jaime Novoa, partner at Kfund.
Zynap is designed to meet the needs of MSSPs and Fortune 2000 companies seeking agile, scalable, and effective tools to defend against cyberattacks. In a landscape where threats constantly evolve, Zynap looks to deliver innovative, market-differentiating solutions.
Its standardised solutions ensure integration with existing tools and frameworks while offering customisation to suit specific client and sector needs. Zynap provides measurable outcomes that empower clients to make informed decisions.
Additionally, by leveraging generative AI alongside other advanced technologies, Zynap reportedly delivers precise results that enable businesses to protect themselves from cybercriminal threats even before they occur.
Zynap’s team is currently developing a trial version of its product, set to be released in the coming months.

36 Best Ai Startups in Spain to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Spain
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 36 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $740.3m. The average funding per company in this subset is $20.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.
Last update to the database: Mar 17, 2026. See changelog.

Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.

evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Share post

36 Best Ai Startups in Spain to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Spain
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 36 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $740.3m. The average funding per company in this subset is $20.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.
Last update to the database: Mar 17, 2026. See changelog.

About us
Our mission is to empower insurance companies, providing them with tailor-made experiences for the needs of their current and future clients
Image 6Image 7
Image 9Image 10
Image 12Image 13
Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.
Our values
Think big & be bold
dreaming of a grand Bdeo.
Always go the extra mile
seeking excellence.

and grow together.

supporting_evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Functional Cookies
[x] Functional Cookies

Targeting Cookies

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.

Reduce costes. Acelera procesos.
La Inteligencia Visual de Bdeo permite la captura de imágenes a través de un smartphone, para luego examinarlas y detectar daños y otra información de forma instantánea.
Ofrece una estimación de costes y ayuda a la aseguradora o compañía de flotas a acelerar la toma de decisiones: puede proponer una póliza, realizar un triaje, automatizar el pago de indemnizaciones o agilizar la subida de evidencia adicional desde el taller.
Todo, en cuestión de minutos.

Image 3Image 4
About us
Our mission is to empower insurance companies, providing them with tailor-made experiences for the needs of their current and future clients
Image 6Image 7
Image 9Image 10
Image 12Image 13
Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.
Our values
Think big & be bold
dreaming of a grand Bdeo.
Always go the extra mile
seeking excellence.
We care about each other
and grow together.
Laser-focused on customer success
building a strong relationship.

supporting_evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!
