About Bdeo - The power to change insurance industry

   Property Insurers

Image 3Image 4
About us
Our mission is to empower insurance companies, providing them with tailor-made experiences for the needs of their current and future clients
Image 6Image 7
Image 9Image 10
Image 12Image 13
Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.
Our values
Think big & be bold
dreaming of a grand Bdeo.
Always go the extra mile
seeking excellence.
We care about each other
and grow together.
Laser-focused on customer success
building a strong relationship.

supporting_evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.

Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Log into your account your username your password Forgot your password? Password recovery Recover your password your email Search Magazine Interviews Re-/Launch Funding Acquisitions Other Stuff Know-How Summit Event Overview Exhibition Options Sponsorship Options Get Tickets Job Board Job Board Post a Job Insights Startup Sourcing Premium Reports Podcast Database Startup Database Investor Database About us Mission / Team Advertising Our Newsletter – CLUB – Register Now Log In CLUB Benefits Search Magazine Interviews Re-/Launch Funding Acquisitions Other Stuff Know-How Summit Event Overview Exhibition Options Sponsorship Options Get Tickets Job Board Job Board Post a Job Insights Startup Sourcing Premium Reports Podcast Database Startup Database Investor Database About us Mission / Team Advertising Our Newsletter – CLUB – Register Now Log In CLUB Benefits Search Home Funding Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI Funding Spain-Startups Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI By Stefano De Marzo June 5, 2024 Share Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Linkedin Alinia AI , an alignment platform enabling companies’ safe and controlled deployment of generative AI, guided by their policies and business preferences, announces a successful €2.2 million pre-seed funding round. Speedinvest and Precursor led the round with participation from KFund and notable angels including Clem Délangue & Thom Wolf (Hugging Face Co-Founders), Tom Preston (GitHub Co-Founder), Xavier Amatriain (Google Core AI VP), and Oriol Vinyals (Google DeepMind VP of Research). Significant adoption of generative AI is underway with spending set to hit $143 billion by 2027, but its rapid adoption comes with significant challenges. The problem? Managing control and safety in the face of risks to prevent inappropriate or brand-damaging content. Additionally, navigating upcoming regulatory efforts like the EU AI Act, Biden´s Executive Order, or Canada´s Bill C-27, which demand rigorous safety checks and clear documentation, also adds hurdles to mass enterprise adoption. Evaluating the performance and reliability of generative AI applications from both safety and business dimensions is a top obstacle for the enterprise today. This is especially challenging for companies when leveraging advanced techniques like fine-tuning and RAG, maintaining LLM versions, and coordinating across teams including machine learning, governance, and business domain experts. “The technology is not production-ready for business scenarios. Enterprise leaders are rightfully afraid of making headlines because their generative AI applications go rogue. Reputational harm keeps them up at night,” said Ariadna Font Llitjós, co-founder & CEO of Alinia. “Using a process known as Alignment, we ensure AI systems adhere to expected behaviors, policies, and regulatory requirements – helping enterprises seize the promise of this new technology while minimizing the risks and potential harm.” Founded in 2023, Alinia Alignment Platform aims to provide an integral AI governance approach – which currently requires multiple tools – ranging from evaluation and real-time monitoring to cutting-edge optimization techniques, coupled with documentation features facilitating compliance at every stage of the LLM lifecycle. Enabling customers to have clear and accurate evidence of how LLM-powered applications behave in different enterprise scenarios, for specific tasks, and abiding by specific rules, is the first step towards alignment for the enterprise. “ In my experience at Hugging Face, governance and safety aspects of LLM development require an incredible amount of time and effort. But when you are about to release a foundation model that will be used millions of times, governance and safety are non-negotiables ,” addedCarlos Muñoz Ferrandis, co-founder & COO. With an initial focus on enabling subject-matter experts to guide and validate the performance and safety of their generative AI applications, Alinia will use the funding to hire additional talent to build the Alinia Alignment Platform to create an end-to-end alignment process focused on safety and regulatory requirements – enabling safe and inclusive use of generative AI across LLM modalities and languages. Fred Hagenauer, Partner with Speedinvest, said: “ Ari and Carlos have some of the best profiles we´ve seen to tackle the alignment problem in the generative AI space. Their experience and knowledge of the early days of enterprise AI, RAI, and the LLM governance required today is something truly unique. For us, it was a no-brainer to be the first ones to support them.” Charles Hudson, Managing Partner with Precursor Ventures, commented: “Alinia brings the confidence that enterprise lacks today to deploy generative AI in a safe and trustworthy way. Being able to control and steer these powerful agents is such a challenge, but they have the experience needed and are willing to take it head-on.” Tags alinia ai barcelona gen ai kfund pre-seed round precursor ventures SpeedInvest Share Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Linkedin Previous article Manchester-based Ittybit snaps €1.7 million to make robust media workflows accessible to developers Next article Emerging Trends: Europe’s next big tech opportunities Stefano De Marzo Stefano De Marzo is the former Head of News at EU-Startups. He has been extensively covering startups, venture capital and innovation ecosystems, including contributions to numerous publications such as Sifted, Entrepreneur and Forbes. Through his work as an editor and writer, he continues to shape the narrative surrounding the best stories of the tech world. Madrid-based startup Shakers raises €14 million to help businesses integrate teams of specialised talent with AI
By David Cendon Garcia
Share
FacebookTwitterWhatsAppLinkedin
HRTech startup Shakers, a Spanish workforce management platform connecting companies with tech freelancers, today announced its €14 million Series A funding round in order to expand double its headcount.
The funding round was led by Partech, with further investment from KFund, Brighteye Ventures, Athos Capital and Wayra.
Héctor Mata, Shakers CEO, said: “The convergence of AI and independent talent is not a trend – it’s a new operating model. Demand for specialised technical talent is soaring, but the traditional outsourcing model hasn’t evolved in over decades. With Shakers, we are setting a new standard for hiring: our platform acts as the new operating system for performance-driven freelancers — giving them the same benefits as traditional roles, while offering companies the agility to innovate faster. As we expand across Europe, we’re building the infrastructure layer that powers the AI-infused workplace of the future.”
Founded in 2021, Shakers is a workforce orchestration platform that connects businesses with specialised tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents. Founded by four serial entrepreneurs – Héctor Mata (CEO), Nico de Luis (COO), Adrián de Pedro (CPO) and Jaime Castillo (CRO) – with backgrounds in AI and software development, and first-hand experience in both finding freelance work and hiring talent.
With over 10,000 vetted freelancers and 450+ client companies including Microsoft, Inditex and Telefónica, Shakers aims to empower a new generation of performance-driven freeworkers with integrated services such as banking, insurance and productivity tools – all within its all-in-one Freelancer OS.
The company has grown over 6x in revenue over the past year, and is aiming to continue on its growth trajectory. 60% of their revenue reportedly comes from recurring customers and 30% of revenue generated organically from other European markets.
Shakers envisions a future of work where businesses can easily onboard specialised teams of tech freelancers and autonomous AI agents to collaborate, experiment, and drive new technological capabilities via complex and long-term projects. The platform empowers businesses and freelancers to automate their most “frustrating” workflow challenges and upgrade their productivity skills, promoting quick and effective results.
According to data provided by Shakers, this comes at a time when the traditional IT outsourcing landscape is being reshaped, with the European market projected to grow from €180 billion to over €280 billion by 2030 as demand shifts toward more flexible, high-skill talent solutions.
Shakers plans to double its team size in the next year to facilitate their growth, while expanding its AI agent integrations for freelancers and businesses. By 2027, Shakers will reportedly have orchestrated over 10,000 hybrid projects.
Simone Riva, Partech Senior Principal, said: “At Partech, we believe that while AI is reshaping the way we work, exceptional talent will always be at the heart of the future of work. From our very first conversation, it was clear the Shakers team is building something special — a fast, intuitive platform tailored to the flexible workforce of tomorrow. They’re redefining how companies find, hire, and collaborate with top talent. We’re proud to back this visionary team and are excited to support them as they scale across Spain, Europe, and beyond.”
As part of Shakers’ community of 10,000+ tech freelancers, they will enjoy the same benefits as traditional employment, including embedded services for banking, insurance, productivity and more, alongside the flexibility, freedom and incentives of self-employment.
According to Shakers, their technology goes beyond just sourcing – it validates talent end-to-end, assessing both hard and soft skills, experience, and cultural fit through more than 1,000 data points.
Integrated with Shakers Verified, their pre-vetting tool, the platform eliminates the need for early interviews and technical tests, delivering ready-to-work talent from day one. This means that companies can potentially go from idea to fully assembled team in minutes.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
36 Best Ai Startups in Spain to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Spain
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 36 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $740.3m. The average funding per company in this subset is $20.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.
Last update to the database: Mar 17, 2026. See changelog.

About Bdeo - The power to change insurance industry

Image 3Image 4
About us
Our mission is to empower insurance companies, providing them with tailor-made experiences for the needs of their current and future clients
Image 6Image 7
Image 9Image 10
Image 12Image 13
Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.
Our values
Think big & be bold
dreaming of a grand Bdeo.
Always go the extra mile
seeking excellence.
We care about each other
and grow together.
Laser-focused on customer success
building a strong relationship.

Propuesta de próximos pasos
4

Actualizaciones desde  el taller
Nosotros. Impulsores en la transformación de las aseguradoras
El 65% del equipo de Bdeo está dedicado a la constante evolución de nuestra tecnología, la más avanzada de su tipo en el mercado. Siempre vamos un paso más allá y nuestros valores acompañan a nuestro producto allí donde vaya.
Saber más
Agilización
Innovación
Confianza

Casos de éxito
Lo que haces es lo que eres. Por eso dejamos que nuestros clientes os cuenten un poco más sobre Bdeo.
Saber más

¿Quieres conocer nuestra tecnología más a fondo?
Solicita una demo y descubre de primera mano todos los beneficios de Bdeo.
Empieza hoy [...] Más de 60 compañías en 25 países ya utilizan Bdeo.
Reduce costes. Acelera procesos.
La Inteligencia Visual de Bdeo permite la captura de imágenes a través de un smartphone, para luego examinarlas y detectar daños y otra información de forma instantánea.
Ofrece una estimación de costes y ayuda a la aseguradora o compañía de flotas a acelerar la toma de decisiones: puede proponer una póliza, realizar un triaje, automatizar el pago de indemnizaciones o agilizar la subida de evidencia adicional desde el taller.
Todo, en cuestión de minutos.
Productos adaptados a cada industria
Aseguradoras de autos
Acelera procesos y reduce el fraude desde la suscripción de un nuevo vehículo hasta el  
cierre de la gestión de un siniestro.
Gestión de siniestrosSuscripción de pólizas [...] Inspección y evaluación de daños en segundos.
Agiliza la toma de decisiones con recomendaciones inteligentes que dan superpoderes a tu equipo. Todo gracias a la tecnología más avanzada de Inteligencia Visual.
Empieza hoy
Nuestra tecnología optimiza los procesos de tu compañía aseguradora o de gestión de flotas para lograr una experiencia de usuario avanzada. Precisión, flexibilidad y fiabilidad  
para ayudarte en tu toma de decisiones y mejorar el ratio combinado.
Tus procesos con Bdeo
Gestión: De 3 semanas a 5 días.
Inspección: De 5 días a 5 minutos.
Reduce hasta un 16% los costes de reparación de siniestros.
Aumenta hasta en un 15% la detección de fraude.
Reduce hasta un 70% los costes directos de suscripción.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Passes the 50 Client Mark

Bdeo, the company specialising in Visual Intelligence that optimises and automates insurance processes, ended last year with 50 clients in its portfolio. This growth has allowed the company to expand its presence to three continents, including Europe, Latin America and Africa, as well as in more than 25 countries, five more than a year ago. It currently has offices and/or representation in Spain, Mexico, the United Kingdom, France and Italy.
Commitment to the Spanish insurance market
More than half of all car insurance subscriptions in Spain are now processed using Bdeo’s technology. This milestone was reached in 2022, with Bdeo partnering with 8 of the top 10 motor insurers in Spain.
The company’s main clients in Spain include Mapfre, Mutua Madrileña, Zurich, AXA, Reale, Admiral, Generali, Catalana Occidente, Lagun Aro, Santalucía, Verti, Kutxabank Seguros, Liberty, Zurich and Mussap.
Transforming insurance processes in Latin America
In Latin America, Bdeo’s technology has also brought significant evolution to the insurance sector. It has helped reduce policy underwriting inspection times to less than 5 minutes; reduced inspection times for small claims to less than 8 minutes, and has taken economic claims estimation times from 40 minutes to 15 minutes.
This has enabled total repair times to be reduced from 24 hours in best-case scenarios to 5 hours or less. Bdeo’s customers in the region include Allianz, Banorte, BBVA, BCI, Cesvi, Chubb, HDI, La Caja, Mapfre, Asistencia, Seguros Atlas and Zurich. Some of these clients are already using Bdeo’s technology to cover more than 60% of valuations.

The Bdeo team
Doubling the number of processed claims
In 2022, Bdeo’s Artificial Intelligence solutions processed more than 1.5 million cases, double the amount processed during the previous year.
This increase in activity, together with a growing international presence and new achievements in customer service, resulted in Bdeo being recognised as a top 100 Insurtech by Fintech Global, inclusion in Silicon Valley’s Plug&Play programme, and 4YFN awards nomination.
Breakthrough solutions for homes and fleets
In 2022, Bdeo introduced new breakthrough solutions. Following the success of its solution for motor insurers, the company launched a new Artificial Intelligence solution for home insurers that enables the detection and analysis of damp damage and glass damage.
In addition, Bdeo launched Visual De-Fleeting, a comprehensive AI solution that allows fleet companies to optimise their time managing and evaluating large fleets of vehicles. The solution enables fleet managers to assess any vehicle’s condition in seconds, directly from the user’s own mobile device, and can be applied to leasing, renting, subscription, car sharing or rent-a-car business models.
Visual Intelligence: Using AI to assess damage and estimate costs
Bdeo continues to enhance its core Visual Intelligence technology, a specific type of Artificial Intelligence that helps collect visual evidence of vehicle or home damage via any mobile device, then analyse it and estimate policy rates or repair costs in a matter of seconds.
Visual Intelligence helps to automate the underwriting and claims management processes for auto and home insurance companies, as well as inspection processes for fleet management companies, drastically reducing processing times from weeks to minutes. As a result, companies can minimise friction, increase customer satisfaction and reduce operating costs.

The Challenge:  Before Metabase, Bdeo.io used Redash, but many of the non-technical team members struggled with Redash’s user interface and its limitations.
The Solution:  Bdeo needed a platform that could run SQL, but that could also create interactive queries for non-experts. They wanted to build dashboards that told good stories, and wanted a wide choice of visualizations and formats to help tell those stories.
The Results:  Metabase made it easier for Bdeo teams to access their data and make better decisions.

The Visual Intelligence technology developed by Bdeo helps insurance companies automate certain tasks in their underwriting and claims management processes. This allows insurers to shorten their processes, save time and costs, and speed up decision-making to offer a better experience to policyholders. Currently, 48 companies in more than 20 countries use Bdeo’s technology in their processes. Bdeo has a team of 70 people, about 50% of whom are technical teams (data and tech).
Why Metabase?
Bdeo generates a lot of data, from customers, product, artificial intelligence models, etc, and all this information is stored in a relational database to be able to perform queries on structured data. They were looking for a platform for querying and visualizing their data so they could answer questions, spot trends, and monitor the behavior of their data (operational, financial, marketing, and data science).
The data
All this data is directly anonymized, formatted, and propagated from their transactional databases (DynamoDB) to their relational analytical databases (PostgreSQL) following an event-driven approach (triggered with any modification and using AWS Lambda for data propagation).
How Bdeo team uses Metabase
With Metabase, Bdeo built dashboards for:
The main teams that make the most use of Metabase are the data, product, and customer success teams, but the marketing, sales, and finance teams use Metabase as well.
Questions include:
Data team
Product team
Customer success:
The data team makes greater use of SQL queries, as raw SQL offers deeper customization for more complex results; however, the query builder is very helpful for simplifying the query for other teams to explore.
The results
The main advantage of using Metabase is that it has connected the different teams through all the information generated by the company.
“Many of Metabase’s features are remarkable, but we could highlight the variety of visualizations and their customization available for queries.”, - says Javier.
Metabase offers a dynamic SQL query creation interface with the use of variables, snippets, and options to review table information interactively. This SQL editor has allowed people outside the data team to become more interested in how to query data and improve their SQL skills to meet their primary needs.
Javier’s Advice for others
Pay attention to all the features of Metabase’s product before getting crazy generating queries. For instance, the Snippets can help you avoid constantly repeating parts of the queries, and the variable filters can help you generalize queries and make them cross-functional.
Metabase allows you to organize questions and dashboards with collections, so a collections structure by area and project will make it easier for people to find what they’re looking for. You can also mark collections as official, which is important to differentiate validated results. You should put the same effort in extracting data insights as you do in organizing, sharing, and communicating them in an understandable way for the different departments involved in your company.

What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.

supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Madrid-based Bdeo raises €7.5M to deploy AI in insurance industry

Bdeo founders
Based out of Madrid, Bdeo, provides Visual Intelligence for the insurance and fleet industries. It has raised an additional €7.5 million in funding. The new financing came from new investors Armilar, Hollard, The Centre for the Development of Industrial Technology (CDTI), which invested in Zinkler and Toqio, and Íope Ventures, a new investment vehicle launched by Wayra and Telefónica Seguros. Existing investors K Fund and Blackfin also participated in the investment.
Funds to bolster its growth
Bdeo will use the investment to accelerate its international expansion and strengthen its position across Europe and Latin America. While Bdeo already manages more than 50% of motor insurance underwriting and works with 8 of the 10 leading motor insurers in Spain. In addition to accelerating its international expansion, Bdeo plans to continue developing its Visual Intelligence solutions to cover new use cases.
“Based on the impressive results we have achieved and our capital efficiency given the challenging times we’re living in, it is reassuring to receive this trust from investors,” said Julio Pernía, CEO of Bdeo. “It is a significant boost of confidence for our team, and we are proud to continue working closely with the insurance sector and adapting to its needs and priorities, as we always have.”
“Bdeo has successfully created an easy-to-integrate platform that brings together the latest computer vision technologies coupled with proprietary data and intelligence from third-party systems. This assures an immediate impact on the insurer’s bottom line, which has been recognised over time by Bdeo’s clients,” said João Dias, Principal at Armilar.
“At Wayra, we are delighted to invest in Bdeo through Íope Ventures with our partners at Telefónica Seguros. We believe in the disruptive potential of insurtech based on AI, which, as we know, is revolutionising the business ecosystem and, in this case, will enable greater agility and efficiency throughout insurance operations, thus providing a better service to the end user,” said Paloma Castellano, Director of Wayra Madrid.
“We at K Fund believe that Bdeo’s potential goes beyond the insurance industry; its damage detection and cost estimation technology can be applied to other use cases such as vehicle fleet management or property management, use cases that the company is already making progress with. We have no doubt that Bdeo will become one of the major references in artificial intelligence in Europe,” said Marc Clemente, Partner at K Fund.
“We’re more than happy to continue supporting Bdeo in its growth journey. We have immense confidence in Bdeo’s team and are enthusiastic about continuing to collaborate with them on their expansion. Since day one, we have been deeply convinced of the pivotal role that Bdeo’s technology plays in empowering insurers to effectively manage claims and optimise their operational processes,” said Gabrielle Thomas, Investment Director at BlackFin Tech.
Visual Intelligence to transform insurance
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía and Manuel Moreno, two entrepreneurs with experience in building B2B SaaS companies within the insurance industry. It specialises in Visual Intelligence, a specific type of AI that optimises and automates processes for insurance and fleet management companies.
The company’s mission is to transform the auto and home insurance sectors, as well as fleet management, through its Visual Intelligence solutions, changing how customers interact with companies in these sectors. With this value proposition, Bdeo is revolutionising the underwriting and claims assessment processes by minimising friction between the user and the company, increasing user satisfaction, and reducing operational costs for companies.
Widespread presence
Currently, Bdeo works with more than 50 insurers in more than 25 countries. The company has a growing presence across Latin America and Europe, particularly in countries such as Spain, Portugal, Italy, France, Nordic countries, the United Kingdom, Mexico, Colombia, and Argentina.
Bdeo works with global insurance leaders such as Reale, Mapfre, and Generali in Spain, Ageas in Portugal, Fidelidade in Portugal, Zurich, Allianz, and BBVA in the Latin American market, and Hollard in South Africa.

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. 
More information
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Performance Cookies
[x] Performance Cookies 
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Functional Cookies
[x] Functional Cookies 
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Targeting Cookies
[x] Targeting Cookies 
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

company in this subset is $78.5m.

36 Best Ai Startups in Spain to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Spain
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 36 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $740.3m. The average funding per company in this subset is $20.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.
Last update to the database: Mar 17, 2026. See changelog.

The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. supporting_evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Share post

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. 
More information
Allow All
Manage Consent Preferences
Performance Cookies
[x] Performance Cookies 
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Functional Cookies
[x] Functional Cookies 
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Targeting Cookies
[x] Targeting Cookies 
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

About us
Our mission is to empower insurance companies, providing them with tailor-made experiences for the needs of their current and future clients
Image 6Image 7
Image 9Image 10
Image 12Image 13
Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.

Think big & be bold

Propuesta de próximos pasos
4

Actualizaciones desde  el taller
Nosotros. Impulsores en la transformación de las aseguradoras
El 65% del equipo de Bdeo está dedicado a la constante evolución de nuestra tecnología, la más avanzada de su tipo en el mercado. Siempre vamos un paso más allá y nuestros valores acompañan a nuestro producto allí donde vaya.
Saber más
Agilización
Innovación
Confianza

Casos de éxito
Lo que haces es lo que eres. Por eso dejamos que nuestros clientes os cuenten un poco más sobre Bdeo.
Saber más

¿Quieres conocer nuestra tecnología más a fondo?
Solicita una demo y descubre de primera mano todos los beneficios de Bdeo.
Empieza hoy [...] Más de 60 compañías en 25 países ya utilizan Bdeo.
Reduce costes. Acelera procesos.
La Inteligencia Visual de Bdeo permite la captura de imágenes a través de un smartphone, para luego examinarlas y detectar daños y otra información de forma instantánea.
Ofrece una estimación de costes y ayuda a la aseguradora o compañía de flotas a acelerar la toma de decisiones: puede proponer una póliza, realizar un triaje, automatizar el pago de indemnizaciones o agilizar la subida de evidencia adicional desde el taller.
Todo, en cuestión de minutos.
Productos adaptados a cada industria
Aseguradoras de autos
Acelera procesos y reduce el fraude desde la suscripción de un nuevo vehículo hasta el  
cierre de la gestión de un siniestro.
Gestión de siniestrosSuscripción de pólizas [...] Inspección y evaluación de daños en segundos.
Agiliza la toma de decisiones con recomendaciones inteligentes que dan superpoderes a tu equipo. Todo gracias a la tecnología más avanzada de Inteligencia Visual.
Empieza hoy
Nuestra tecnología optimiza los procesos de tu compañía aseguradora o de gestión de flotas para lograr una experiencia de usuario avanzada. Precisión, flexibilidad y fiabilidad  
para ayudarte en tu toma de decisiones y mejorar el ratio combinado.
Tus procesos con Bdeo
Gestión: De 3 semanas a 5 días.
Inspección: De 5 días a 5 minutos.
Reduce hasta un 16% los costes de reparación de siniestros.
Aumenta hasta en un 15% la detección de fraude.
Reduce hasta un 70% los costes directos de suscripción.

36 Best Ai Startups in Spain to Watch in 2026
The Definitive Seedtable Ranking of Ai Startups in Spain
We track 71,000+ companies and rank them dynamically using our Seedtable Score – a score that uses quantitative and qualitative data points to signal the momentum behind a company. We then monitor the list manually leveraging our expertise as founders and investors.
There are 36 start-ups with an aggregate funding of $740.3m. The average funding per company in this subset is $20.6m.
The list encompasses 404 founders. 1863 executives. It includes data on 2267 professionals within these filtered companies.
Last update to the database: Mar 17, 2026. See changelog.

Image 6Image 7
Image 9Image 10
Image 12Image 13
Get to know us a little better
Bdeo was founded in 2017 by Julio Pernía, an entrepreneur with a lot of experience in building B2B Saas companies within the insurance industry. Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. On the other hand, with the fighting spirit of all the members of the team, who work every day to strengthen the links between insurers and their clients.

Think big & be bold
dreaming of a grand Bdeo.

seeking excellence.

supporting_evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Share post
Related Posts

Bdeo and carvaloo Join Forces to Enhance Commercial Fleet Damage Management
13 October 2025 · By Gema Carreño

01 September 2025 · By Gema Carreño

Visual Intelligence applied to the insurance industry
Artificial Intelligence in general, and Visual Intelligence in particular, are very present in our days. Perhaps, much more than we imagine.

Privacy Preference Center
When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. 
More information

Performance Cookies
[x] Performance Cookies 
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Functional Cookies
[x] Functional Cookies 
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Targeting Cookies
[x] Targeting Cookies 
These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
Strictly Necessary Cookies
Always Active
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.
Functional Cookies
[x] Functional Cookies 
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.
Targeting Cookies

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Later, Ruth Puente joined as founder, and little by little more people joined the team, until we became what we are today, a multicultural team based in Spain and Mexico, with extensive experience in the insurance industry.
The team grows and strengthens, on the one hand, with the support of several of the most important European venture capital funds in the Fintech & Insurtech field. supporting_evidence.title: Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech ...
supporting_evidence.snippet:
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged. [...] Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo. [...] If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
supporting_evidence.raw_content:
Bdeo Public Website

Bdeo's Blog
BDEO
Ruth Puente, COO of Bdeo, named Top Women Insurtech Leader by Acord and Alchemy Crew
14 December 2022 · By Ana Navarrina

If you work in the insurance sector, pay attention to this list. The Top Insurtech Leaders, curated each year by Acord together with Alchemy Crew and co-founder Sabine VanderLinden recognises professionals who are leading innovation in the insurance industry through technology.
The list rewards their unique vision, execution and results.
This year 2022, the list stands out once again, with a little twist: they wanted to dedicate the list exclusively to women who are leading the insurance technology industry.
Recently, a groundbreaking report by Congress in the US has shown worrying levels of disparity between men and women in the sector. Therefore, it is more important than ever to honour the invaluable contributions of women in the insurance industry.
Since the release of the 2022 list, Bdoers are celebrating. Our COO, Ruth Puente, has been named Top Insurtech Leader. This recognition for Ruth and our female talent, with international impact, is a dream for a team like ours.
What qualities have the colleagues at Acord and Alchemy Crew valued in order to grant this recognition? Let's take a look at them.
Qualities to be part of Top Insurtech Leaders 2022
Collaboration and cooperation
Teamwork and collaboration are fundamental for an insurtech to have the strength to transform a sector.
Leaders who make it to this list realise that transformative and sustainable innovation will only happen if collaboration between all types of players in the industry is encouraged.
It is essential to share perspectives and to achieve cohesion between the strategic needs of the sector and the technology that is developed to meet them.
Sustainability and inclusion
The insurance industry continues to undergo a digital transformation. Insurers need to find ways to drive change and be part of it, so that they can ensure better care for their policyholders in the present and near future.
Industry leaders, through these technological advances, are also helping to make the industry more accessible and equitable. They are also reinventing traditional processes to make them greener and more sustainable.
Global ecosystem builders
Three accelerators are setting a new pace in the transformation of the insurance industry: robust networks, interconnected systems and global digitalisation.
Those conscious leaders capable of ensuring these are the future of the industry and will even impact beyond it, reaching out to new and diverse sources of expertise and knowledge.
The value of our COO
Ruth joined Bdeo in 2019, in charge of coordinating the company's processes and operations as COO. Since then, she has been part of the company's DNA.
Her experience in the digital and tech world, in the operations team of different startups and companies, is of undeniable value to Bdeo.
But his greatest victory at Bdeo has been to design a unique and differential culture in the international entrepreneurial sector, establishing transparent and solid values and installing practices for employee satisfaction, with tools such as Nailted. As a result, we know that 9 out of 10 employees would recommend Bdeo to a friend as a place to work.
On behalf of all the women who make up Bdeo (one of them writing these lines): thank you Ruth for representing us and giving visibility to female talent!

Share post

supporting_evidence.snippet: Alinia AI, an alignment platform enabling companies' safe and controlled deployment of generative AI, guided by their policies and business preferences,
supporting_evidence.raw_content: Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI | EU-Startups Sign in Magazine Interviews Re-/Launch Funding Acquisitions Other Stuff Know-How Summit Event Overview Exhibition Options Sponsorship Options Get Tickets Job Board Job Board Post a Job Insights Startup Sourcing Premium Reports Podcast Database Startup Database Investor Database About us Mission / Team Advertising Our Newsletter – CLUB – Register Now Log In CLUB Benefits Sign in Welcome! Log into your account your username your password Forgot your password? Password recovery Recover your password your email Search Magazine Interviews Re-/Launch Funding Acquisitions Other Stuff Know-How Summit Event Overview Exhibition Options Sponsorship Options Get Tickets Job Board Job Board Post a Job Insights Startup Sourcing Premium Reports Podcast Database Startup Database Investor Database About us Mission / Team Advertising Our Newsletter – CLUB – Register Now Log In CLUB Benefits Search Magazine Interviews Re-/Launch Funding Acquisitions Other Stuff Know-How Summit Event Overview Exhibition Options Sponsorship Options Get Tickets Job Board Job Board Post a Job Insights Startup Sourcing Premium Reports Podcast Database Startup Database Investor Database About us Mission / Team Advertising Our Newsletter – CLUB – Register Now Log In CLUB Benefits Search Home Funding Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI Funding Spain-Startups Barcelona-based Alinia raises €2.2 million pre-seed to enable safe deployment of genAI By Stefano De Marzo June 5, 2024 Share Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Linkedin Alinia AI , an alignment platform enabling companies’ safe and controlled deployment of generative AI, guided by their policies and business preferences, announces a successful €2.2 million pre-seed funding round. Speedinvest and Precursor led the round with participation from KFund and notable angels including Clem Délangue & Thom Wolf (Hugging Face Co-Founders), Tom Preston (GitHub Co-Founder), Xavier Amatriain (Google Core AI VP), and Oriol Vinyals (Google DeepMind VP of Research). Significant adoption of generative AI is underway with spending set to hit $143 billion by 2027, but its rapid adoption comes with significant challenges. The problem? Managing control and safety in the face of risks to prevent inappropriate or brand-damaging content. Additionally, navigating upcoming regulatory efforts like the EU AI Act, Biden´s Executive Order, or Canada´s Bill C-27, which demand rigorous safety checks and clear documentation, also adds hurdles to mass enterprise adoption. Evaluating the performance and reliability of generative AI applications from both safety and business dimensions is a top obstacle for the enterprise today. This is especially challenging for companies when leveraging advanced techniques like fine-tuning and RAG, maintaining LLM versions, and coordinating across teams including machine learning, governance, and business domain experts. “The technology is not production-ready for business scenarios. Enterprise leaders are rightfully afraid of making headlines because their generative AI applications go rogue. Reputational harm keeps them up at night,” said Ariadna Font Llitjós, co-founder & CEO of Alinia. “Using a process known as Alignment, we ensure AI systems adhere to expected behaviors, policies, and regulatory requirements – helping enterprises seize the promise of this new technology while minimizing the risks and potential harm.” Founded in 2023, Alinia Alignment Platform aims to provide an integral AI governance approach – which currently requires multiple tools – ranging from evaluation and real-time monitoring to cutting-edge optimization techniques, coupled with documentation features facilitating compliance at every stage of the LLM lifecycle. Enabling customers to have clear and accurate evidence of how LLM-powered applications behave in different enterprise scenarios, for specific tasks, and abiding by specific rules, is the first step towards alignment for the enterprise. “ In my experience at Hugging Face, governance and safety aspects of LLM development require an incredible amount of time and effort. But when you are about to release a foundation model that will be used millions of times, governance and safety are non-negotiables ,” addedCarlos Muñoz Ferrandis, co-founder & COO. With an initial focus on enabling subject-matter experts to guide and validate the performance and safety of their generative AI applications, Alinia will use the funding to hire additional talent to build the Alinia Alignment Platform to create an end-to-end alignment process focused on safety and regulatory requirements – enabling safe and inclusive use of generative AI across LLM modalities and languages. Fred Hagenauer, Partner with Speedinvest, said: “ Ari and Carlos have some of the best profiles we´ve seen to tackle the alignment problem in the generative AI space. Their experience and knowledge of the early days of enterprise AI, RAI, and the LLM governance required today is something truly unique. For us, it was a no-brainer to be the first ones to support them.” Charles Hudson, Managing Partner with Precursor Ventures, commented: “Alinia brings the confidence that enterprise lacks today to deploy generative AI in a safe and trustworthy way. Being able to control and steer these powerful agents is such a challenge, but they have the experience needed and are willing to take it head-on.” Tags alinia ai barcelona gen ai kfund pre-seed round precursor ventures SpeedInvest Share Facebook Twitter WhatsApp Linkedin Previous article Manchester-based Ittybit snaps €1.7 million to make robust media workflows accessible to developers Next article Emerging Trends: Europe’s next big tech opportunities Stefano De Marzo Stefano De Marzo is the former Head of News at EU-Startups. He has been extensively covering startups, venture capital and innovation ecosystems, including contributions to numerous publications such as Sifted, Entrepreneur and Forbes.
